name: bash
description:
Execute a shell command in the output directory. Replaces all file inspection and data transformation needs.

Use for: reading/writing files (cat, echo >), inspecting structure (head, wc, ls), JSON processing (jq — filter, reshape, flatten nested objects, extract fields, convert formats), CSV handling (awk, cut, csvtool), text search (grep), sorting (sort), building answer payloads, and creating intermediate files for multi-step pipelines.

Examples: `jq '.[] | {name, age}' data.json`, `jq -r '.results[] | [.lat, .lon] | @csv' api_output.json > coords.csv`, `cat file.json | jq length`, `head -5 data.csv`, `echo '{"answer":42}' > answer.json`.

CWD is the output directory. Use absolute paths to read files outside it. Output is truncated at 20 KB.

command.description: Bash command to execute (supports pipes, redirects, jq, awk, grep, sort, and all standard Unix tools)
